author: @neilorourke71
text: Lol you sound exactly like Rob.  Are you both related?

author: @AdricM
text: yep,  9 times out of 10 when you see a tripod in a thrift store,  the plate is missing.  thingiverse,  thangs, and a few other places are life savers.

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: OHHHH you should print out some parts to make a tripod out of some sticks and maybe even make one out of those paper sticks that Rob made a bicycle frame from!!! then you won't have to fight over the tripod either, one shoe and tripod each, ;) (then you can point your cameras at each other like dueling with pistols at dawn but it will be dueling with cameras at dawn ;) (look up the shoulder pod that is basically a hunting rifle type butstock that a camera mounds into instead of bullets and barrel with the camera lens housing as the "barrel" so you can "shoot" animals as in take quick steady photos of them, by shooting them with the camera XD

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: an idea would be to make some sort of color filters for standard multi temp white lamps for your camera shooting area, then a rotating hanging and platform thing to hand some things and set somethings down while rotating around using say one of those motors from the microwave ovens XD

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: HA I just used layers of plastic and this contact adhesive on wax backing to bond the layers together, (then a nylon screw that fits the bottom of the cameras I have, which is 1/4"-20tpi,)

author: @bobvincent5921
text: Thanks. This version seems to have better torque than a three blade setup where this prime mover is travelling further. The angle to the wind is easily set.

author: @robertpitt8418
text: Thats Facinating luke i was just working last night putting a shoe on my tripod.  I was converting a telescope tripod for my camera .your a lucky man to have that printer , i just spent exactly the amount you said €20 on a cheep tripod stand and tuck the shoe of it . It worked out well , except your now over £19 richer than me  : )

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahaha it is indeed! I keep finding things I want to print but I have to ask myself, "do I need it?" and usually the answer is no LOL.  Thanks mate! :)

author: @Coxeysbodgering
text: Well done, there are only so many ornaments and bits that can be printed, useful and cost saving prints and makes are soo much better

author: @martinX74
text: Magic! Luke Goes from wild to "normal" in One sentence 🤣
